name: The Doppler Effect: Sound Waves
description: Visualizing how a moving sound source creates the Doppler Effect. As a source moves with velocity $v_s$, the wavefronts compress in the direction of motion (higher frequency) and expand in the opposite direction (lower frequency). The observed frequency $f$ is given by $f = f_0 \frac{v}{v \pm v_s}$, where $v$ is the speed of sound.
renderingCode: class ScienceSimulation { constructor(container, initialInputs) { this.container = container; this.canvas = document.createElement('canvas'); this.container.appendChild(this.canvas); this.ctx = this.canvas.getContext('2d'); this.waves = []; this.speedOfSound = 340; window.addEventListener('resize', () => this.resize()); this.resize(); } resize() { this.canvas.width = this.container.clientWidth; this.canvas.height = this.container.clientHeight; } getSteps(inputs) { const vObs = inputs.velocity > 0 ? (this.speedOfSound / (this.speedOfSound - inputs.velocity)) * inputs.freq : inputs.freq; return [ { title: "Setup", description: "The source emits spherical wavefronts at ${inputs.freq} Hz." }, { title: "Wave Compression", description: "As the source moves at ${inputs.velocity} m/s, waves compress ahead, resulting in a higher observed frequency of ~${vObs.toFixed(1)} Hz." } ]; } render(inputs, stepIndex) { const ctx = this.ctx; const w = this.canvas.width; const h = this.canvas.height; ctx.fillStyle = '#0f172a'; ctx.fillRect(0, 0, w, h); const time = Date.now() / 1000; if (Math.random() < 0.1) { this.waves.push({ x: w / 2, t: time }); } this.waves = this.waves.filter(wave => (time - wave.t) < 2); const sourceX = (w / 2) + (Math.sin(time) * inputs.velocity * 0.5); ctx.strokeStyle = '#3b82f6'; ctx.lineWidth = 3; this.waves.forEach(wave => { const dt = time - wave.t; const radius = dt * this.speedOfSound * 0.5; const centerX = wave.x + (inputs.velocity * dt * 0.5); ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(centerX, h / 2, radius, 0, Math.PI * 2); ctx.stroke(); }); ctx.fillStyle = '#f87171'; ctx.beginPath(); ctx.arc(sourceX, h / 2, 10, 0, Math.PI * 2); ctx.fill(); requestAnimationFrame(() => this.render(inputs, stepIndex)); } }
